personal.name: Marshanda Ayu Puspita
personal.title: Electronics Engineer
personal.tagline: Designing circuits that power the future.
personal.bio: Electronics Engineer with a passion for embedded systems, PCB design, and signal processing. I bridge the gap between hardware and software to build reliable, efficient systems.

projects.title: Wireless Sensor Network for Industrial Monitoring
projects.description: Designed a mesh-topology wireless sensor network using ESP32 modules to monitor temperature, humidity, and vibration in real-time for a factory environment. Data transmitted via MQTT to a cloud dashboard.

projects.title: Custom BLDC Motor Controller
projects.description: Developed a 3-phase BLDC motor controller from scratch with FOC algorithm, using STM32 and custom gate driver circuitry. Achieved 96% efficiency at rated load.

projects.title: Low-Power IoT Environmental Logger
projects.description: Built an ultra-low-power data logger for remote environmental sensing. Solar-charged with battery backup, LoRa communication, and 2-year estimated battery life in sleep mode.

projects.title: Analog ECG Signal Conditioner
projects.description: Designed a precision instrumentation amplifier chain for ECG acquisition: INA128 front-end, notch filter at 50Hz, bandpass filter 0.5–150Hz, and USB data capture.

projects.title: Automated PCB Testing Fixture
projects.description: Created an automated bed-of-nails test fixture with Python-controlled relay matrix to verify PCB functionality during production. Reduced testing time by 70%.

projects.title: MPPT Solar Charge Controller
projects.description: Implemented Perturb & Observe MPPT algorithm on a 12V/10A solar charge controller with synchronous buck converter, LCD status display, and protection circuits.

experience.description: Lead hardware design for embedded IoT products. Responsible for PCB layout, firmware architecture, and production testing protocols.
Supported R&D team in designing and validating power electronics boards. Conducted EMC pre-compliance testing and schematic reviews.
Assisted in developing microcontroller-based automation systems. Wrote firmware for sensor acquisition and implemented data visualization in Python.
GPA 3.75/4.00. Thesis: 'Design and Implementation of Wireless Sensor Node for Structural Health Monitoring using LoRa Technology.'

testimonials.name: Budi Santoso
testimonials.text: A highly detail-oriented engineer with deep understanding of both analog and digital systems. Consistently delivers reliable hardware designs on schedule.

testimonials.name: Dr. Rina Wulandari
testimonials.text: One of the most motivated students I have supervised. Demonstrated exceptional ability to translate theoretical knowledge into practical, working systems.

testimonials.name: Ahmad Fauzan
testimonials.text: Brought a strong engineering mindset to our product development process. The PCB testing fixture he built cut our QC time dramatically.

blog.title: Why PCB Impedance Matching Matters More Than You Think
blog.excerpt: A deep dive into transmission line effects on high-speed PCB traces and how to calculate the right trace geometry for your stackup.

blog.title: Getting Started with FreeRTOS on STM32
blog.excerpt: A practical guide to setting up FreeRTOS tasks, queues, and semaphores on the STM32 platform using STM32CubeIDE.

blog.title: Understanding Decoupling Capacitors: Placement & Value Selection
blog.excerpt: Why decoupling caps are often misunderstood, and a systematic approach to selecting values and placement for power integrity.
